name: fmd
description: Foundational Matrix Documents — the factory. Consumes a seed set (idea.md + optional brand/market siblings + a context block, authored upstream e.g. by the idea-kit) and generates a /docs set sized to the build. FMD is standalone: it does not require or run problem/market validation. Doc templates are unnumbered slugs; generation order lives in dependsOn, not filenames.
versionNote: 4.1.0 — added the INV-### invariant spine, IDE-agnostic keeper, pitch cluster, and competition context; schema realigned to idea-forge 2.1.0 (ADR-0005). 4.2.0 — fixed doc-drift defects, added EARS + the Iron Law, and delegated code execution to the coding layer (ADR-0006). 4.3.0 — upgraded the existing conditional implementation-plan into a living execution state with stable TASK-### IDs, dependency-derived waves, event-owned checkpoints, team branch/PR/conflict guidance, deterministic plan integrity check T6, and conditional Playwright guidance. 4.4.0 — added preview-first GitHub Issue/Project projection. 4.5.0 — adds a fact-only Build Keeper/run-evidence loop, Framework Observer proposals, run-scoped GitHub activation with digest-bound apply, and two suite reviews. Markdown remains canonical; no GitHub Action/workflow is emitted. No idea.md/context contract change (ADR-0009).

seedSet.note: FMD consumes a SEED SET, not one file. idea.md is the problem-pure, F-### core (required). Optional siblings authored upstream: brand (feeds design-system), market (feeds mrd/gtm), and a context block (sizes the doc set). idea.md keeps solution-purity + the ID spine; siblings carry solution/brand/market so idea.md stays clean. See IdeaForge/FMD-CONTRACT.md.

inputContract.note: FMD grounds generation in whatever the seed set contains. A structural preflight (ORCHESTRATOR.md step 1) only checks the brief has the sections the selected docs read; it never refuses to run or judges the idea.

contextIntake.when: orchestrator step 0, before any doc is selected or generated
contextIntake.purpose: Sizes the doc set to the build (CJ's project-scale rules). Authored upstream in the seed set, or asked once at factory start.

selectionModel.auto: Rules propose the doc set from the context fields; human confirms or overrides once. Default; right for a real build under time pressure.
selectionModel.learning: Human proposes the doc set WITH REASONING; FMD critiques each doc against a rubric (cost=tokens/time, value=which decision it de-risks, need=does this build require it). The critique is explicitly ALLOWED and EXPECTED to affirm a correct set; it must never invent docs to justify itself.

process.purpose: Pipeline; marks the forge/factory boundary and the seed-set input.
Pipeline diagram + the generator-verifier QA loop + the reconcile pass.

templates.definition: The intake block that sizes the doc set: team, time, build type, judged, computes-numbers, exposed-surface, selection mode.

The problem-pure seed brief: problem, segment, feature set with F-### IDs, and §9 invariants with INV-### IDs. The traceability origin for both features and guardrails. Schema aligned to idea-forge 2.1.0.

templates.condition: a design-system or judged UI is in scope
templates.definition: Optional seed sibling: brand identity, tone, color, typography. Grounds the design-system doc. Authored upstream, not generated by FMD.

templates.condition: mrd or gtm is in scope
templates.definition: Optional seed sibling: market sizing, competitors, positioning evidence. Grounds mrd/gtm. Authored upstream, not generated by FMD.
Emitted docs/index.md. §0 source-of-truth map (one fact, one home) built from the docs actually generated; the anti-context-poisoning contract.

templates.condition: company/large scale AND not collapsed into a north-star
templates.definition: Business Requirements: why the business wants this (objectives, scope, stakeholders, risks).

templates.condition: company/large scale AND not collapsed into a north-star
templates.definition: Market Requirements: who the market is, size, alternatives, positioning. Grounds in the market seed sibling.
Product Requirements: what we build — features (F-###), user journeys (UJ-###), business rules (BR-###), and hard must-never invariants (INV-###). The backbone.

templates.condition: maturing/company scale
templates.definition: Functional Requirements: precisely how each feature must behave.

templates.condition: high-rigor/company scale
templates.definition: Software Requirements Spec: full formal requirements (rare; high-rigor builds).
System Design: how the system is architected — components, data flow, trade-offs.

templates.condition: maturing/company scale
templates.definition: Technical Design: low-level algorithms, function signatures, detailed design.

templates.condition: the product exposes an API
templates.definition: API Spec: the contracts (API-###) other code/clients depend on.
Data Model: schema, entities, relationships.

templates.condition: product computes or derives numbers (scores, prices, fees, on-chain amounts, risk metrics, projections)
templates.definition: Glass-box methods ledger: every computed number = EQ-### equation + DS-### inputs + confidence. The LLM narrates/cites, never originates a number.

templates.condition: a UI is in scope; grounds in the brand seed sibling
templates.definition: Design System: UI tokens, components, states, routes & actions. Grounds in the brand seed sibling.
QA Test Plan: the traceability sink — every F-### gets >=1 test, every INV-### gets >=1 negative test, and each automated case names its command/path; browser UI gets conditional Playwright guidance.

templates.condition: any network-exposed surface
templates.definition: Security & Compliance: auth, authz, data privacy, legal for exposed surfaces.

templates.condition: business/adoption/market/release planning is actually required (not judged=true alone)
templates.definition: Release / GTM: how we ship and pitch it. Grounds in the market seed sibling.

templates.condition: a build crew is wanted (most builds that will actually be coded by agents)
templates.definition: Subagents Document: the 3-6 build-time agent roster; materializes to .claude/agents/*.md. Model tiers fast/balanced/deep.

templates.condition: a build crew / tracked implementation is wanted
templates.definition: Living execution state (spec->code bridge): stable TASK-### tasks, dependency-derived waves, owner/write-scope/work-ref/status/gate evidence, event-triggered checkpoints, run evidence, and an optional GitHub Issue/Project projection. No separate tasks.md.

templates.condition: new contributor, handoff, or explicit onboarding need
templates.definition: Onboarding: orient a new human/agent in minutes. Points to canonical docs, never restates.

templates.condition: a feature has a non-obvious design choice worth recording (used even in fast builds)
templates.definition: RFC: lightweight per-feature design/decision note, scoped to an F-###; retires into the PRD/ADR.

templates.condition: mode=team OR time_budget > ~1 day (Draft->Locked->Superseded lifecycle)
templates.definition: Change Record (CR-###): append-only log of a change to a Locked doc. Governance for team/multi-day builds only.

templates.condition: outlives_demo=true
templates.definition: Ops & observability runbook: deploy, config/secrets, monitoring, incidents, backup/recovery. Only if it runs past the demo.

templates.condition: after the build/demo (human-filled)
templates.definition: Postmortem: blameless retro aimed at improving the kit and the next build. Feeds long-term memory.
ADR: append-only record of a significant decision's rationale (supersede, never edit).

templates.condition: team, multi-session build, or expected material pivots (not judged=true alone)
templates.definition: Append-only, trunk-only decision history: pivots, rejected approaches, names/immutable IDs, decision assumptions, and INV-### audits. Product behavior/architecture/tests/execution remain in PRD/system design/QA/implementation plan. Emitted to the build repo and kept honest by hooks + AGENTS.md; never freezes the spec.

templates.condition: judged=true
templates.definition: Pitch kit (filled instance of playbooks/pitch-deck-playbook): narrative, BMC→beat map, rubric-mapped timed script, Q&A ownership map, interview probe sheet. Consumes the rubric from the context block; never restates weights.

docTypeDefinitionsNote: Each template carries a one-line `definition` (pinned per ADR-0004) so a slug never drifts in meaning now that numbering is gone. The emitted index.md §0 echoes the definitions of the docs actually generated.
docSetSelection: Start from mvpDocSet, then add every template whose `condition` holds for the context block. Do NOT generate all templates by default. North-star collapse: below team/company scale, generate ONE vision doc instead of separate brd+mrd.

playbooks.purpose: How the context intake sizes the doc set per build.
The VC pitch canon re-weighted for a judged rubric (method); the filled instance is templates/pitch-kit.md. Consumes the rubric from the context block.

agents.purpose: How to run the factory. Read first. Never emitted.
Filled and emitted to the target root as AGENTS.md; includes the conditional living-plan checkpoint and task branch/PR/conflict protocol.
Scoped *.mdc stack-currency guardrail: verify framework APIs against pinned-version docs, never from memory.
Materialized from the SAD doc. The product's build-time agent roster (Claude Code / Cursor).

modes: reconcile (propagate a human/teammate edit across referencing docs; report contradictions)

tools.purpose: Deterministic T6 structural gate: strict contiguous task rows, unique exact IDs/dependencies, valid states and required fields, resolved acyclic dependency DAG, exact trace refs, and explicit observed result/artifact evidence outside commands for reviewed/done work. On a valid plan it also emits the topological execution waves and flags same-wave write-scope overlaps as parallel-safety warnings (advisory; failing under --strict).

tools.runtime: python3 stdlib + gh CLI (only when GitHub delivery is configured)
tools.purpose: Run-scoped, preview-first projection of the canonical implementation-plan TASK-### ledger into one marker-backed GitHub Issue per task and terminal-manageable GitHub Project fields. --apply requires the exact reviewed manifest SHA; GitHub state never silently rewrites Markdown.
Appends validated fact-only events to docs/run-evidence.jsonl; unknowns remain unknown and run evidence never gates the lead's authority to build.

qaLoop.humanizer: explicit request or externally published prose only; style after facts

reconcile.trigger: a task is claimed/blocked/reviewed/merged/cut; a decision or pivot is accepted; implementation discovers a material dependency or failed assumption; time/team/scope changes; or a session/milestone ends
reconcile.action: run the implementation-plan checkpoint transaction: observe code/tests, update stable TASK-### state and evidence, update canonical docs only when owned truth changed, run consistency reconcile for affected IDs, recompute ready/blocked/parallel/cut work, and run tools/check-implementation-plan.py. Significant decisions still go to the decision ledger/ADR.
reconcile.note: Build-level self-reconciliation. The implementation plan owns execution state; index.md §0 keeps product/architecture/test/decision facts in their canonical docs. Kit-level learning remains post-build retro + curated framework change.

forge.name: idea-kit
forge.status: OPTIONAL upstream — not required by FMD, not part of FMD
forge.produces: the seed set (idea.md + optional brand/market siblings + context block) that FMD consumes
forge.note: The idea-kit is one optional way to author the seed set (and validate the problem, if wanted). FMD does NOT require it, run its validation, or vendor its validator. FMD consumes any seed set matching the input contract. Seed-set authoring changes tracked in IdeaForge/FINDINGS-seed-refactor.md. Decoupling recorded in adr/ADR-0002; seed set + intake in adr/ADR-0004.

examples.purpose: One complete v4.0 worked reference: context + idea.md -> selected /docs set (unnumbered slugs, index §0 map) -> emitted product AGENTS.md.
